description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front panel and wooden side panels, giving it a classic and robust appearance. The front features a horizontal blue-lit frequency display, a large tuning knob on the right, and several smaller control knobs and switches below the display for volume, balance, and other audio adjustments. The top surface is metallic with two sets of ventilation slits for heat dissipation. The rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, indicating its use as an audio component in a home or garage setting. The sides are finished in a warm wood veneer, adding to its retro aesthetic. The overall build suggests a solid, durable construction typical of mid-to-late 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a brushed metal front, blue-lit frequency display, multiple control knobs, and wooden side panels. The rear features RCA ports and speaker terminals, while the top has ventilation slits.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency display, large tuning knob, several smaller control knobs, and switches, all set in a brushed metal panel with wooden sides.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the wooden panel and the metallic top with ventilation slits, with control knobs slightly visible at the front edge.
description_view_2: The rear view displays multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, and the continuation of the wooden side panels.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wooden panel and metallic top, with the large tuning knob slightly protruding at the front.
